calendar.matters:
DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Nov-03-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Lawrence Bazel 415-402-2700 of Briscoe Ivester & Bazel LLP appearing for plaintiff.
Hayley Peterson, Deputy Attorney General, 619-738-9311 appearing via Zoom for defendant.

Mr. Bazel contests tentative ruling. Argued. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT, LAWRENCE GOLDZBAND DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Leslie Salt Co. v. San Francisco Bay Conservation & Development Commission (1984) 153 Cal.App.3d 605.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the court's tentative ruling and in compliance with compliance CRC 3.1312(b)Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Oct-12-2023 Continued To Nov-03-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501

DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Sep-21-2023 Continued To Oct-19-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

RESPONDENT'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is continued to October 19, 2023 on Court's own motion.

Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Aug-25-2023 Continued To Sep-21-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

RESPONDENTS' DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is continued to September 21, 2023 for the parties to comply with SFRL 2.7B and deliver courtesy copies of their respective filings in support of and in opposition to the motion with cover letters reflecting the new hearing date no later than August 31, 2023. (Courtesy copy of the moving papers received).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
